news.headline: Global military spending hits record $2.9 trillion in 2025 amid growing insecurity
news.summary: Global military spending climbed to nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking an 11th straight year of growth as rising insecurity and rearmament boosted defence budgets, researchers said on Monday. The United States, China and Russia accounted for $1.48 trillion, over half the total, SIPRI said.

news.headline: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
news.summary: Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

news.summary: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.

news.headline: Trump factor drives up Asia-Pacific military spending at fastest pace since 2009
news.summary:
Military spending in the Asia-Pacific rose at the fastest pace for 16 years in 2025 as US allies felt “growing uncertainty” over whether Washington would honour its security commitments, according to a new report.
Total global spending reached US$2.89 trillion, an increase of 2.9 per cent from 2024,

news.summary: Naftali Bennett, a right-wing politician, and Yair Lapid, a centrist, will merge parties for a vote later this year.

news.headline: Syria begins first trial of Bashar al-Assad and allies after his ouster
news.summary: A Syrian court held its first hearing on Sunday in the trial of ousted president Bashar al-Assad and senior officials from his former government, state media said. news.summary: Political violence has escalated in Colombia ahead of next month's presidential election.
